revision.comment: Bot: mark 1 deleted QID(s) as DELETED_QID in ill templates [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24017044590|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:姉崎古墳群]]
{{Infobox ancient site | name = Anesaki Kofun Group | alternate_name = | image = [[File:Anesaki Futago-zuka Kofun, zenkei.JPG|280px]] | caption = [[Anesaki Futago-zuka Kofun]] {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=280|qq=}} | location ={{ill|Ichihara, Chiba|en|Ichihara, Chiba|qid=Q207881|lt=Ichihara, Chiba|1=Ichihara, Chiba}} {{ill|Anesaki|ja|姉崎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anesaki|1=Anesaki district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q27500978}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en|Chiba Prefecture|qid=Q80011|lt=Chiba Prefecture|1=Chiba Prefecture}}, Japan; {{ill|Anesaki|qid=Q28686258|1=Anesaki}}| affiliation = | coordinates = | coordinates = 35 | coordinates = 28 | coordinates = 15.4 | coordinates = 140 | coordinates = 03 | coordinates =05.2; {{Coord|35.47094444444444|N|140.05144444444446|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| omitted = JP-12 | type = Keyhole-shaped, round, and square kofun | built = Late {{ill|4th century|en|4th century|qid=Q8099|lt=4th century|1=4th century}} to late {{ill|7th century|en|7th century|qid=Q8089|lt=7th century|1=7th century}} | Burial Facility = Corridor-style stone chambers, etc. | area = 34 tombs | buried_person = Possibly the {{ill|Shanghai-kamiko|ja|上海上国造|1=Kamitsu Unakami no Kuni Miyatsuko|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shanghai-kamiko|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsu Unakami no Kuni Miyatsuko|qq=}} clan<ref>Chiba Prefecture History Materials Archaeology Vol. 2, 2003, pp. 682-683, 686.</ref> | burial goods_found = | historic_sites = {{ill|Anesaki Futago-zuka Kofun|ja|姉崎二子塚古墳|1=Anesaki Futagozuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anesaki Futago-zuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Anesaki Futagozuka Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Anegasaki Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|姉崎天神山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anesaki Tenjinyama Kofun|qq=}}, etc. | Historic Site1_cultural_properties = | registered_cultural_properties = | archaeological_site_id = | burial goods_Historic Site = | notes = | map = Japan Chiba | map = | map_dot_label = Anesaki Kofun Group | map_dot_label = bottom }} The '''Anesaki Kofun Group''' ({{ill|Anesaki Kofun Group|ja|姉崎古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anesaki Kofun Group|qq=}}, Anesaki Kofun-gun) is a cluster of {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|qid=Q1141225|lt=Kofun|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofuns) located along the southern banks of the {{ill|Yōrō River|en|Yōrō River|qid=Q8062667|lt=Yōrō River|1=Yōrō River}} in {{ill|Ichihara, Chiba|en|Ichihara, Chiba|qid=Q207881|lt=Ichihara, Chiba|1=Ichihara, Chiba}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en|Chiba Prefecture|qid=Q80011|lt=Chiba Prefecture|1=Chiba Prefecture}}. The kofun date from the late {{ill|4th century|en|4th century|qid=Q8099|lt=4th century|1=4th century}} to the late {{ill|7th century|en|7th century|qid=Q8089|lt=7th century|1=7th century}} and are situated on alluvial plains and terraces at elevations ranging from 30 to 40 meters.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Walking through Bōsō's Kofun - Ichihara City’s Anesaki Kofun Group (Kofun on the Left Bank of the Lower Yōrō River)|publisher=Shibayama Kofun and Haniwa Museum|url=https://www.haniwakan.com/kenkyu/boso/anesaki.html|accessdate=2020-01-13|qq=}}</ref> == Overview == A total of 34 kofun have been identified within the Anesaki Kofun Group, including 11 keyhole-shaped kofun, 2 square-keyhole kofun, 20 round kofun, and 1 square kofun. Many of the original mounds have been lost over time. Among the most prominent structures is the {{ill|Anegasaki Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|姉崎天神山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anesaki Tenjinyama Kofun|qq=}}, the largest early-period keyhole-shaped kofun in the {{ill|Bōsō Peninsula|en|Bōsō Peninsula|qid=Q1022115|lt=Bōsō Peninsula|1=Bōsō Peninsula}} region, measuring 130 meters in length. Nearby, the {{ill|Anesaki Futago-zuka Kofun|ja|姉崎二子塚古墳|1=Anesaki Futagozuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anesaki Futago-zuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Anesaki Futagozuka Kofun|qq=}} stands on sandy plains and measures 103 meters in length. Approximately 600 meters west of the Anesaki Tenjinyama Kofun lies the {{ill|Anesaki Shrine|ja|姉埼神社|sv|Anesaki-jinja|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anesaki Shrine|qq=}}, believed to have been established by the {{ill|Shanghai-kamiko|ja|上海上国造|1=Kamitsu Unakami no Kuni Miyatsuko|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shanghai-kamiko|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsu Unakami no Kuni Miyatsuko|qq=}} clan, a local ruling family. South of the shrine is the 86-meter-long Shakazan Kofun, while the now-destroyed 69-meter-long Sannōzan Kofun once stood roughly 100 meters to the west. The {{ill|Rokusonōbara Kofun|ja|六孫王原古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokusonōbara Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=DELETED_QID|1=Q135500938}}, located 600 meters south of the Anesaki Tenjinyama Kofun, is a square-keyhole-shaped mound with a corridor-style stone chamber facing south. The earliest kofun in this group is believed to be the Anesaki Tenjinyama Kofun, dating to the early to mid-{{ill|4th century|en|4th century|qid=Q8099|lt=4th century|1=4th century}}. This was followed by the construction of the Shakazan Kofun in the late {{ill|4th century|en|4th century|qid=Q8099|lt=4th century|1=4th century}}. The middleperiod is represented by the Anesaki Futago-zuka Kofun, dating to the late {{ill|5th century|en|5th century|qid=Q8095|lt=5th century|1=5th century}}. However, few middle-period keyhole-shaped kofun have been identified in the region. During the late period, the Sannōzan Kofun (early {{ill|6th century|en|6th century|qid=Q8090|lt=6th century|1=6th century}}) and the Rokusonōbara Kofun (late {{ill|7th century|en|7th century|qid=Q8089|lt=7th century|1=7th century}}) were constructed. By the late period, the decline of prominent local chiefs such as the Shanghai-kamiko clan is evident in the lack of substantial funerary goods and the reduced map_size of the kofun. == notes == {{Footnotes|qq=}} {{Reflist|qq=}} == References == * Terumasa Maesawa, ''Kofun of the East Country - A Treasury of Ancient History,'' Society Publishing, 1985, ISBN 4-88169-314-X, pp. 49-51. == External Links == * [http://www.haniwakan.com/kenkyu/boso/anesaki.html Shibayama Kofun and Haniwa Museum: Anesaki Kofun Group in Ichihara City (Kofun on the Left Bank of the Lower Yōrō River)] {{Japan-history-stub|qq=}}
{{Kofun navbar|qq=}}
{{translated page|ja|姉崎古墳群|version=97992678|comment=Imported full ja history}}
